title: Angioimmunoblastic T-Cell Lymphoma — first line

/try.html dropdown coverage. PRELIMINARY — NOT clinically signed off.

groups.title: Demographics + baseline status
groups.description: Base fields. ECOG 4 → engine routes to palliative track.

questions.label: ECOG Performance Status
questions.helper: ECOG 4 → engine will not propose active treatment.

questions.label: Line of therapy
questions.helper: This questionnaire targets 1L. For 2L+ use the extended JSON mode.

groups.title: Biomarkers (auto-extracted from indications)
groups.description: Fields auto-generated from biomarker_requirements in disease Indications. To be refined by clinician.

questions.label: CD30 expression by IHC
questions.helper: Source: KB BIO-CD30-IHC.

questions.label: RHOA G17V mutation
questions.helper: Engine routes through this biomarker if positive. Source: KB BIO-RHOA-G17V.

groups.title: Baseline safety (virology + blood)
groups.description: Screening before systemic therapy. Engine uses this for RF-HBV-REACTIVATION-RISK etc.

questions.label: HBsAg
questions.helper: HBsAg+ → entecavir prophylaxis before anti-CD20/anti-CD38/cytotoxic immunosuppressive.

groups.title: Additional parameters
groups.description: Fill in what is available; the rest — engine will fall back to safe defaults.

questions.label: Creatinine clearance (CrCl, mL/min)
questions.helper: <30 → renal-impaired regimens; <60 → dose modify many cytotoxics.

questions.label: Bilirubin × ULN
questions.helper: >1.5× ULN → hepatic-impairment regimens.

questions.label: ANC (K/μL)
questions.helper: <1.0 K/μL — engine will add G-CSF prophylaxis.

questions.label: Platelets (K/μL)
questions.helper: <75 — many regimens dose-modify; <50 — hold most cytotoxics.

groups.title: General screening (optional)
groups.description: Universal fields for fitness assessment, infection status, and organ function. Fill in whatever is in the chart — empty fields are simply not evaluated by the engine (replaces 'unevaluated RedFlag' warnings).

questions.label: LVEF (%) — echocardiography
questions.helper: Before anthracyclines / HER2 agents / alkylating agents — baseline LVEF required.

questions.label: Active tuberculosis?
questions.helper: Screening before IO / steroids / alloSCT. Quantiferon or screening chest X-ray.

questions.label: Active uncontrolled infection?
questions.helper: Any sepsis / pneumonia / uncontrolled fungal infection — treatment delay required.

questions.label: Total bilirubin (× ULN)
questions.helper: Most regimens require bili ≤1.5×ULN; FDA approvals commonly express this in multiples of ULN.

questions.label: DLCO (% of predicted)
questions.helper: DLCO <50% — bleomycin contraindicated (cHL ABVD); <40% — caution with CAR-T.

questions.label: QTc (ms) on ECG
questions.helper: QTc >480 → caution with venetoclax, crizotinib, apalutamide, FLT3i.

questions.label: Uric acid
questions.helper: Elevated level + bulky disease → TLS risk, rasburicase required.

questions.label: Number of significant comorbidities
questions.helper: Heart failure / COPD / cirrhosis / CKD / diabetes etc. Driver for frailty score.

questions.label: Charlson Comorbidity Index
questions.helper: 0-1 fit, 2-3 intermediate, ≥4 frail (for elderly + solid tumours).

questions.label: G8 Geriatric Screening (0-17)
questions.helper: ≤14 — full geriatric assessment required before intensive chemotherapy.

questions.label: Child-Pugh class (liver)
questions.helper: Only if cirrhosis / liver disease present. B/C — excludes most regimens.

questions.label: Rapid progression (rapid volume increase / new symptoms)?
questions.helper: Driver for intensification in aggressive lymphomas / CAR-T bridging.

questions.label: New metastatic disease on repeat scan?
Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy (CTCAE)
questions.helper: Grade ≥3 — bortezomib SC, vincristine, oxaliplatin, cisplatin contraindicated.

questions.label: NYHA class (heart failure)
questions.helper: Only if HF history present. NYHA III/IV — anthracyclines / trastuzumab contraindicated.

questions.label: WBC (×10⁹/L)
questions.helper: Hyperleukocytosis (>50-100) — risk of leukostasis in AML/ALL/CML-blast.

questions.label: Uncontrolled hypertension?
questions.helper: Contraindicated / use with caution with VEGF inhibitors (bevacizumab, sunitinib, lenvatinib).

notes: STUB auto-generated 2026-04-27. Required: clinician review of biomarker question wording, defaults, missing disease-specific risk factors.

questions.helper: Engine routes through this biomarker if positive. Source: KB BIO-BCR-ABL1.

groups.title: Baseline safety (virology + blood)
groups.description: Screening before systemic therapy. Engine uses this for RF-HBV-REACTIVATION-RISK etc.

questions.label: HBsAg
questions.helper: HBsAg+ → entecavir prophylaxis before anti-CD20/anti-CD38/cytotoxic immunosuppressive.

groups.title: Additional parameters
groups.description: Fill in what is available; the rest — engine will fall back to safe defaults.

questions.label: Creatinine clearance (CrCl, mL/min)
questions.helper: <30 → renal-impaired regimens; <60 → dose modify many cytotoxics.

questions.label: Bilirubin × ULN
questions.helper: >1.5× ULN → hepatic-impairment regimens.

questions.label: ANC (K/μL)
questions.helper: <1.0 K/μL — engine will add G-CSF prophylaxis.

questions.label: Platelets (K/μL)
questions.helper: <75 — many regimens dose-modify; <50 — hold most cytotoxics.

groups.title: General screening (optional)
groups.description: Universal fields for fitness assessment, infection status, and organ function. Fill in whatever is in the chart — empty fields are simply not evaluated by the engine (replaces 'unevaluated RedFlag' warnings).

questions.label: LVEF (%) — echocardiography
questions.helper: Before anthracyclines / HER2 agents / alkylating agents — baseline LVEF required.

questions.label: Active tuberculosis?
questions.helper: Screening before IO / steroids / alloSCT. Quantiferon or screening chest X-ray.

questions.label: Active uncontrolled infection?
questions.helper: Any sepsis / pneumonia / uncontrolled fungal infection — treatment delay required.

questions.label: Total bilirubin (× ULN)
questions.helper: Most regimens require bili ≤1.5×ULN; FDA approvals commonly express this in multiples of ULN.

questions.label: DLCO (% of predicted)
questions.helper: DLCO <50% — bleomycin contraindicated (cHL ABVD); <40% — caution with CAR-T.

questions.label: QTc (ms) on ECG
questions.helper: QTc >480 → caution with venetoclax, crizotinib, apalutamide, FLT3i.

questions.label: Uric acid
questions.helper: Elevated level + bulky disease → TLS risk, rasburicase required.

questions.label: Number of significant comorbidities
questions.helper: Heart failure / COPD / cirrhosis / CKD / diabetes etc. Driver for frailty score.

questions.label: Charlson Comorbidity Index
questions.helper: 0-1 fit, 2-3 intermediate, ≥4 frail (for elderly + solid tumours).

questions.label: G8 Geriatric Screening (0-17)
questions.helper: ≤14 — full geriatric assessment required before intensive chemotherapy.

questions.label: Child-Pugh class (liver)
questions.helper: Only if cirrhosis / liver disease present. B/C — excludes most regimens.

questions.label: Rapid progression (rapid volume increase / new symptoms)?
questions.helper: Driver for intensification in aggressive lymphomas / CAR-T bridging.

questions.label: New metastatic disease on repeat scan?
Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy (CTCAE)
questions.helper: Grade ≥3 — bortezomib SC, vincristine, oxaliplatin, cisplatin contraindicated.

questions.label: NYHA class (heart failure)
questions.helper: Only if HF history present. NYHA III/IV — anthracyclines / trastuzumab contraindicated.

questions.label: WBC (×10⁹/L)
questions.helper: Hyperleukocytosis (>50-100) — risk of leukostasis in AML/ALL/CML-blast.

questions.label: Uncontrolled hypertension?
questions.helper: Contraindicated / use with caution with VEGF inhibitors (bevacizumab, sunitinib, lenvatinib).

notes: STUB auto-generated 2026-04-27. Required: clinician review of biomarker question wording, defaults, missing disease-specific risk factors.

groups.title: Demographics + baseline status
groups.description: Base fields. ECOG 4 → engine routes to palliative track.

questions.label: ECOG Performance Status
questions.helper: ECOG 4 → engine will not propose active treatment.

questions.label: Line of therapy
questions.helper: This questionnaire targets 1L. For 2L+ use the extended JSON mode.

groups.title: Baseline safety (virology + blood)
groups.description: Screening before systemic therapy. Engine uses this for RF-HBV-REACTIVATION-RISK etc.

questions.label: HBsAg
questions.helper: HBsAg+ → entecavir prophylaxis before anti-CD20/anti-CD38/cytotoxic immunosuppressive.

groups.title: Additional parameters
groups.description: Fill in what is available; the rest — engine will fall back to safe defaults.

questions.label: Creatinine clearance (CrCl, mL/min)
questions.helper: <30 → renal-impaired regimens; <60 → dose modify many cytotoxics.

questions.label: Bilirubin × ULN
questions.helper: >1.5× ULN → hepatic-impairment regimens.

questions.label: ANC (K/μL)
questions.helper: <1.0 K/μL — engine will add G-CSF prophylaxis.

questions.label: Platelets (K/μL)
questions.helper: <75 — many regimens dose-modify; <50 — hold most cytotoxics.

groups.title: General screening (optional)
groups.description: Universal fields for fitness assessment, infection status, and organ function. Fill in whatever is in the chart — empty fields are simply not evaluated by the engine (replaces 'unevaluated RedFlag' warnings).

questions.label: LVEF (%) — echocardiography
questions.helper: Before anthracyclines / HER2 agents / alkylating agents — baseline LVEF required.

questions.label: Active tuberculosis?
questions.helper: Screening before IO / steroids / alloSCT. Quantiferon or screening chest X-ray.

questions.label: Active uncontrolled infection?
questions.helper: Any sepsis / pneumonia / uncontrolled fungal infection — treatment delay required.

questions.label: Total bilirubin (× ULN)
questions.helper: Most regimens require bili ≤1.5×ULN; FDA approvals commonly express this in multiples of ULN.

questions.label: DLCO (% of predicted)
questions.helper: DLCO <50% — bleomycin contraindicated (cHL ABVD); <40% — caution with CAR-T.

questions.label: QTc (ms) on ECG
questions.helper: QTc >480 → caution with venetoclax, crizotinib, apalutamide, FLT3i.

questions.label: Uric acid
questions.helper: Elevated level + bulky disease → TLS risk, rasburicase required.

questions.label: Number of significant comorbidities
questions.helper: Heart failure / COPD / cirrhosis / CKD / diabetes etc. Driver for frailty score.

questions.label: Charlson Comorbidity Index
questions.helper: 0-1 fit, 2-3 intermediate, ≥4 frail (for elderly + solid tumours).

questions.label: G8 Geriatric Screening (0-17)
questions.helper: ≤14 — full geriatric assessment required before intensive chemotherapy.

questions.label: Child-Pugh class (liver)
questions.helper: Only if cirrhosis / liver disease present. B/C — excludes most regimens.

questions.label: Rapid progression (rapid volume increase / new symptoms)?
questions.helper: Driver for intensification in aggressive lymphomas / CAR-T bridging.

questions.label: New metastatic disease on repeat scan?
Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy (CTCAE)
questions.helper: Grade ≥3 — bortezomib SC, vincristine, oxaliplatin, cisplatin contraindicated.

questions.label: NYHA class (heart failure)
questions.helper: Only if HF history present. NYHA III/IV — anthracyclines / trastuzumab contraindicated.

questions.label: WBC (×10⁹/L)
questions.helper: Hyperleukocytosis (>50-100) — risk of leukostasis in AML/ALL/CML-blast.

questions.label: Uncontrolled hypertension?
questions.helper: Contraindicated / use with caution with VEGF inhibitors (bevacizumab, sunitinib, lenvatinib).

notes: STUB auto-generated 2026-04-27. Required: clinician review of biomarker question wording, defaults, missing disease-specific risk factors.

title: Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia / SLL — first line
intro: Questionnaire for CLL/SLL with iwCLL treatment indication. Algorithm distinguishes standard-risk (BTKi continuous, default) vs high-risk cytogenetics (venetoclax + obinutuzumab time-limited, alternative preferred). 6-8 minutes.

groups.title: Demographics + iwCLL treatment indication
groups.description: Asymptomatic CLL without iwCLL indication = active surveillance, not treatment.

questions.label: Age
triggers: filter:age in choice of intensive vs less intensive (FCR limited to ≤65)
questions.helper: Age + IGHV mutated → possibility of FCR (rarely used now). Age ≥65 → BTKi or VenO virtually always.

questions.label: ECOG Performance Status
questions.helper: ECOG 4 — palliative track only; engine will not propose active treatment.

questions.label: Eligible for chemoimmunotherapy (FCR/BR)?
questions.helper: In 2025 chemoimmunotherapy has been displaced by BTKi/venetoclax. Field retained for historical compatibility — for most 1L cases set to false.

questions.label: iwCLL treatment indication present?
questions.helper: At least one of: progressive cytopenia (Hb<10, plt<100), bulky/ progressive splenomegaly or lymphadenopathy, B-symptoms, autoimmune cytopenia not on steroids, lymphocyte doubling time <6 months. WITHOUT this → active surveillance, no plan.

groups.title: Cytogenetic + molecular risk (defines BTKi vs VenO)
groups.description: del(17p), TP53 mut, IGHV-unmutated, complex karyotype = high-risk composite.

questions.label: CLL composite high-risk?

options.label: High-risk (≥1 of: TP53 mut / del17p / IGHV-unmutated / complex karyotype)

questions.helper: Engine per NCCN recommends VenO for high-risk (BTKi does not resensitize del17p; fixed-duration is advantageous).

questions.label: TP53 mutation
questions.helper: ~5-7% newly-diagnosed CLL; makes FCR/BR contraindicated.

questions.label: del(17p) by FISH
questions.helper: ~5-10% 1L; worst single marker.

questions.label: IGHV-unmutated (≥98% germline homology)
questions.helper: ~50% CLL — unmutated = adverse, mutated = favorable.

questions.label: Complex karyotype (≥3 abnormalities)
questions.helper: ≥5 = highly complex, associated with poor prognosis.

groups.title: Cardiac safety (BTKi-specific)
groups.description: BTKi (acalabrutinib/zanubrutinib) — atrial fibrillation, hypertension.

questions.label: History of atrial fibrillation?
questions.helper: AFib history = relative contraindication to BTKi (especially ibrutinib). Acalabrutinib/zanubrutinib safer but not zero risk. VenO has no cardiac risk.

questions.label: High bleeding risk (anticoagulation, GI bleed history)?
questions.helper: BTKi ↑ bleeding risk ~2-3×. Anticoagulant interaction issue.

groups.title: TLS risk (VenO-specific)
groups.description: Venetoclax ramp-up — TLS prophylaxis required.

questions.label: Absolute lymphocyte count (K/μL)
questions.helper: >25 K/μL = high TLS risk → 5-week ramp-up with hospitalization weeks 1-2.

questions.label: Lymph nodes ≥5 cm?
questions.helper: Bulky disease = TLS high-risk irrespective of ALC.

groups.title: HBV + opportunistic infections
groups.description: BTKi + venetoclax — risk of HSV/VZV, less commonly PJP.

questions.label: HBsAg
questions.helper: Obinutuzumab (anti-CD20) — HBV reactivation risk. Engine requires entecavir prophylaxis if HBsAg+.

groups.title: General screening (optional)
groups.description: Universal fields for fitness assessment, infection status, and organ function. Fill in whatever is in the chart — empty fields are simply not evaluated by the engine (replaces 'unevaluated RedFlag' warnings).

questions.label: Active tuberculosis?
questions.helper: Screening before IO / steroids / alloSCT. Quantiferon or screening chest X-ray.

groups.title: Demographics + baseline status
groups.description: Base fields. ECOG 4 → engine routes to palliative track.

questions.label: ECOG Performance Status
questions.helper: ECOG 4 → engine will not propose active treatment.

questions.label: Line of therapy
questions.helper: This questionnaire targets 1L. For 2L+ use the extended JSON mode.

groups.title: Biomarkers (auto-extracted from indications)
groups.description: Fields auto-generated from biomarker_requirements in disease Indications. To be refined by clinician.

questions.label: HER2 status (solid tumors — gastric/GEJ/CRC scoring)
questions.helper: Source: KB BIO-HER2-SOLID.

questions.label: PD-L1 Combined Positive Score (CPS)
questions.helper: Source: KB BIO-PDL1-CPS.

groups.title: Baseline safety (virology + blood)
groups.description: Screening before systemic therapy. Engine uses this for RF-HBV-REACTIVATION-RISK etc.

questions.label: HBsAg
questions.helper: HBsAg+ → entecavir prophylaxis before anti-CD20/anti-CD38/cytotoxic immunosuppressive.

groups.title: Additional parameters
groups.description: Fill in what is available; the rest — engine will fall back to safe defaults.

questions.label: Creatinine clearance (CrCl, mL/min)
questions.helper: <30 → renal-impaired regimens; <60 → dose modify many cytotoxics.

questions.label: Bilirubin × ULN
questions.helper: >1.5× ULN → hepatic-impairment regimens.

questions.label: ANC (K/μL)
questions.helper: <1.0 K/μL — engine will add G-CSF prophylaxis.

questions.label: Platelets (K/μL)
questions.helper: <75 — many regimens dose-modify; <50 — hold most cytotoxics.

groups.title: General screening (optional)
groups.description: Universal fields for fitness assessment, infection status, and organ function. Fill in whatever is in the chart — empty fields are simply not evaluated by the engine (replaces 'unevaluated RedFlag' warnings).

questions.label: LVEF (%) — echocardiography
questions.helper: Before anthracyclines / HER2 agents / alkylating agents — baseline LVEF required.

questions.label: Active tuberculosis?
questions.helper: Screening before IO / steroids / alloSCT. Quantiferon or screening chest X-ray.

questions.label: Active uncontrolled infection?
questions.helper: Any sepsis / pneumonia / uncontrolled fungal infection — treatment delay required.

questions.label: Total bilirubin (× ULN)
questions.helper: Most regimens require bili ≤1.5×ULN; FDA approvals commonly express this in multiples of ULN.

questions.label: DLCO (% of predicted)
questions.helper: DLCO <50% — bleomycin contraindicated (cHL ABVD); <40% — caution with CAR-T.

questions.label: QTc (ms) on ECG
questions.helper: QTc >480 → caution with venetoclax, crizotinib, apalutamide, FLT3i.

questions.label: Uric acid
questions.helper: Elevated level + bulky disease → TLS risk, rasburicase required.

questions.label: Number of significant comorbidities
questions.helper: Heart failure / COPD / cirrhosis / CKD / diabetes etc. Driver for frailty score.

questions.label: Charlson Comorbidity Index
questions.helper: 0-1 fit, 2-3 intermediate, ≥4 frail (for elderly + solid tumours).

questions.label: G8 Geriatric Screening (0-17)
questions.helper: ≤14 — full geriatric assessment required before intensive chemotherapy.

questions.label: Child-Pugh class (liver)
questions.helper: Only if cirrhosis / liver disease present. B/C — excludes most regimens.

questions.label: Rapid progression (rapid volume increase / new symptoms)?
questions.helper: Driver for intensification in aggressive lymphomas / CAR-T bridging.

questions.label: New metastatic disease on repeat scan?
Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy (CTCAE)
questions.helper: Grade ≥3 — bortezomib SC, vincristine, oxaliplatin, cisplatin contraindicated.

questions.label: NYHA class (heart failure)
questions.helper: Only if HF history present. NYHA III/IV — anthracyclines / trastuzumab contraindicated.

questions.label: WBC (×10⁹/L)
questions.helper: Hyperleukocytosis (>50-100) — risk of leukostasis in AML/ALL/CML-blast.

questions.label: Uncontrolled hypertension?
questions.helper: Contraindicated / use with caution with VEGF inhibitors (bevacizumab, sunitinib, lenvatinib).

notes: STUB auto-generated 2026-04-27. Required: clinician review of biomarker question wording, defaults, missing disease-specific risk factors.

groups.description: Fields auto-generated from biomarker_requirements in disease Indications. To be refined by clinician.

questions.label: Alpha-fetoprotein (serum)
questions.helper: Source: KB BIO-AFP.

questions.label: HBV status (HBsAg + anti-HBc serology)
questions.helper: Source: KB BIO-HBV-STATUS.

questions.label: HCV status (anti-HCV antibody screening)
questions.helper: Source: KB BIO-HCV-STATUS.

groups.title: Baseline safety (virology + blood)
groups.description: Screening before systemic therapy. Engine uses this for RF-HBV-REACTIVATION-RISK etc.

questions.label: HBsAg
questions.helper: HBsAg+ → entecavir prophylaxis before anti-CD20/anti-CD38/cytotoxic immunosuppressive.

groups.title: Additional parameters
groups.description: Fill in what is available; the rest — engine will fall back to safe defaults.

questions.label: Creatinine clearance (CrCl, mL/min)
questions.helper: <30 → renal-impaired regimens; <60 → dose modify many cytotoxics.

questions.label: Bilirubin × ULN
questions.helper: >1.5× ULN → hepatic-impairment regimens.

questions.label: ANC (K/μL)
questions.helper: <1.0 K/μL — engine will add G-CSF prophylaxis.

questions.label: Platelets (K/μL)
questions.helper: <75 — many regimens dose-modify; <50 — hold most cytotoxics.

groups.title: General screening (optional)
groups.description: Universal fields for fitness assessment, infection status, and organ function. Fill in whatever is in the chart — empty fields are simply not evaluated by the engine (replaces 'unevaluated RedFlag' warnings).

questions.label: LVEF (%) — echocardiography
questions.helper: Before anthracyclines / HER2 agents / alkylating agents — baseline LVEF required.

questions.label: Active tuberculosis?
questions.helper: Screening before IO / steroids / alloSCT. Quantiferon or screening chest X-ray.

questions.label: Active uncontrolled infection?
questions.helper: Any sepsis / pneumonia / uncontrolled fungal infection — treatment delay required.

questions.label: Total bilirubin (× ULN)
questions.helper: Most regimens require bili ≤1.5×ULN; FDA approvals commonly express this in multiples of ULN.

questions.label: DLCO (% of predicted)
questions.helper: DLCO <50% — bleomycin contraindicated (cHL ABVD); <40% — caution with CAR-T.

questions.label: QTc (ms) on ECG
questions.helper: QTc >480 → caution with venetoclax, crizotinib, apalutamide, FLT3i.

questions.label: Uric acid
questions.helper: Elevated level + bulky disease → TLS risk, rasburicase required.

questions.label: Number of significant comorbidities
questions.helper: Heart failure / COPD / cirrhosis / CKD / diabetes etc. Driver for frailty score.

questions.label: Charlson Comorbidity Index
questions.helper: 0-1 fit, 2-3 intermediate, ≥4 frail (for elderly + solid tumours).

questions.label: G8 Geriatric Screening (0-17)
questions.helper: ≤14 — full geriatric assessment required before intensive chemotherapy.

questions.label: Child-Pugh class (liver)
questions.helper: Only if cirrhosis / liver disease present. B/C — excludes most regimens.

questions.label: Rapid progression (rapid volume increase / new symptoms)?
questions.helper: Driver for intensification in aggressive lymphomas / CAR-T bridging.

questions.label: New metastatic disease on repeat scan?
Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy (CTCAE)
questions.helper: Grade ≥3 — bortezomib SC, vincristine, oxaliplatin, cisplatin contraindicated.

questions.label: NYHA class (heart failure)
questions.helper: Only if HF history present. NYHA III/IV — anthracyclines / trastuzumab contraindicated.

questions.label: WBC (×10⁹/L)
questions.helper: Hyperleukocytosis (>50-100) — risk of leukostasis in AML/ALL/CML-blast.

questions.label: Uncontrolled hypertension?
questions.helper: Contraindicated / use with caution with VEGF inhibitors (bevacizumab, sunitinib, lenvatinib).

notes: STUB auto-generated 2026-04-27. Required: clinician review of biomarker question wording, defaults, missing disease-specific risk factors.

title: HCV-associated Marginal Zone Lymphoma — first line
intro: Questionnaire for confirmed HCV-associated MZL in a patient without prior lymphoma treatment. Completion takes 5-7 minutes. Fields marked CRITICAL are required; without them the engine will issue a warning instead of a Plan.

groups.title: Demographics + general status
groups.description: Basic patient profile.

questions.label: Patient ID (synthetic)
questions.helper: Any non-personal ID — for session history.

questions.label: Age
triggers: filter:age_max constraints on BR-aggressive (none for HCV-MZL, but affects frailty assessment)
questions.helper: Indirect effect — patients ≥75 more likely to receive antiviral-only track.

questions.label: Sex
questions.helper: Does not affect the HCV-MZL 1L algorithm. Collected for the render layer.

questions.label: ECOG Performance Status

options.label: 2 — ambulatory and capable of self-care, up >50% of time

questions.helper: ECOG > 2 excludes BR-aggressive track — engine will not propose chemo, only antiviral monotherapy.

questions.label: Eligible for chemoimmunotherapy
questions.helper: Clinician assesses overall fitness (comorbidities, cardiac and renal function, discussion with patient). False → BR-aggressive track is filtered out.

groups.title: HCV status (defining biomarker)
groups.description: HCV is the etiological driver of this lymphoma.

questions.label: HCV RNA (PCR)
questions.helper: HCV RNA positive — basis for antiviral-first track. If negative, engine falls back to BR-aggressive (standard approach without etiological tool).

questions.label: HCV RNA positive (boolean — for engine clause)
questions.helper: Technical field duplicating the biomarker for evaluation. Auto-filled from the previous question — true if positive.

questions.label: Decompensated cirrhosis?
questions.helper: Child-Pugh B/C — blocks glecaprevir/pibrentasvir DAA. Engine will select sof/vel + RBV alternative (if HCV positive) OR defer antiviral therapy pending hepatology consultation.

groups.title: Tumor burden + phenotype
groups.description: Whether indolent / bulky / aggressive — basis for track selection.

questions.label: Largest nodal mass (cm)
triggers: RF-BULKY-DISEASE at ≥7 cm → switches to BR-aggressive
questions.helper: Bulky (≥7 cm) = automatically BR-aggressive track even if HCV positive. Antiviral-only is too slow to reduce tumor burden.

questions.label: Mediastinal-thoracic ratio (mediastinal mass / thoracic diameter)
questions.helper: Alternative bulky disease criterion — by CT thorax.

questions.label: Severe B-symptoms (fever >38°C, drenching night sweats, weight loss >10% over 6 months)?
questions.helper: Severe B-symptoms — relative indication for chemotherapy even without bulky disease.

questions.field: findings.Indolent presentation (non-bulky, asymptomatic or minimally symptomatic)
questions.label: Indolent presentation (summary: non-bulky + minimal symptoms)?
questions.helper: Technical field for algorithm clause. True if NOT bulky AND NOT severe B-symptoms. Auto-filled from preceding questions.

groups.title: Histology + transformation risk
groups.description: Exclude transformation to DLBCL.

questions.label: Biopsy shows DLBCL transformation?
questions.helper: If transformation to DLBCL is confirmed — engine issues a warning and redirects to the DLBCL-specific algorithm (not HCV-MZL). Currently fallback — BR-aggressive as safest option.

questions.label: LDH ratio to ULN
questions.helper: LDH > 2× ULN — surrogate marker of transformation.

questions.label: Clinical progression over last X weeks (rapid worsening)?
questions.helper: Time from first notice of new mass / symptoms to visit. <12 weeks + high LDH = suspicion of transformation.

groups.title: HBV screening (mandatory if anti-CD20 planned)
groups.description: Any BR-aggressive regimen contains rituximab → HBV reactivation risk.

questions.label: HBsAg
questions.helper: HBsAg positive without prophylaxis — rituximab is contraindicated. Engine activates CI-HBV-NO-PROPHYLAXIS contraindication.

questions.label: Anti-HBc total
questions.helper: Anti-HBc+ HBsAg- = occult HBV → monitoring + prophylaxis per NCCN.

groups.title: General screening (optional)
groups.description: Universal fields for fitness assessment, infection status, and organ function. Fill in whatever is in the chart — empty fields are simply not evaluated by the engine (replaces 'unevaluated RedFlag' warnings).

questions.helper: Before anthracyclines / HER2 agents / alkylating agents — baseline LVEF required.

questions.label: Active tuberculosis?
questions.helper: Screening before IO / steroids / alloSCT. Quantiferon or screening chest X-ray.

questions.label: Active uncontrolled infection?

questions.label: Total bilirubin (× ULN)

questions.label: Child-Pugh class (liver)
questions.helper: Only if cirrhosis / liver disease present. B/C — excludes most regimens.

questions.label: Rapid progression (rapid volume increase / new symptoms)?
questions.helper: Driver for intensification in aggressive lymphomas / CAR-T bridging.

groups.description: Base fields. ECOG 4 → engine routes to palliative track.

questions.label: ECOG Performance Status
questions.helper: ECOG 4 → engine will not propose active treatment.

questions.label: Line of therapy
questions.helper: This questionnaire targets 1L. For 2L+ use the extended JSON mode.

groups.title: Baseline safety (virology + blood)
groups.description: Screening before systemic therapy. Engine uses this for RF-HBV-REACTIVATION-RISK etc.

questions.label: HBsAg
questions.helper: HBsAg+ → entecavir prophylaxis before anti-CD20/anti-CD38/cytotoxic immunosuppressive.

groups.title: Additional parameters
groups.description: Fill in what is available; the rest — engine will fall back to safe defaults.

questions.label: Creatinine clearance (CrCl, mL/min)
questions.helper: <30 → renal-impaired regimens; <60 → dose modify many cytotoxics.

questions.label: Bilirubin × ULN
questions.helper: >1.5× ULN → hepatic-impairment regimens.

questions.label: ANC (K/μL)
questions.helper: <1.0 K/μL — engine will add G-CSF prophylaxis.

questions.label: Platelets (K/μL)
questions.helper: <75 — many regimens dose-modify; <50 — hold most cytotoxics.

groups.title: General screening (optional)
groups.description: Universal fields for fitness assessment, infection status, and organ function. Fill in whatever is in the chart — empty fields are simply not evaluated by the engine (replaces 'unevaluated RedFlag' warnings).

questions.label: LVEF (%) — echocardiography
questions.helper: Before anthracyclines / HER2 agents / alkylating agents — baseline LVEF required.

questions.label: Active tuberculosis?
questions.helper: Screening before IO / steroids / alloSCT. Quantiferon or screening chest X-ray.

questions.label: Active uncontrolled infection?
questions.helper: Any sepsis / pneumonia / uncontrolled fungal infection — treatment delay required.

questions.label: Total bilirubin (× ULN)
questions.helper: Most regimens require bili ≤1.5×ULN; FDA approvals commonly express this in multiples of ULN.

questions.label: DLCO (% of predicted)
questions.helper: DLCO <50% — bleomycin contraindicated (cHL ABVD); <40% — caution with CAR-T.

questions.label: QTc (ms) on ECG
questions.helper: QTc >480 → caution with venetoclax, crizotinib, apalutamide, FLT3i.

questions.label: Uric acid
questions.helper: Elevated level + bulky disease → TLS risk, rasburicase required.

questions.label: Number of significant comorbidities
questions.helper: Heart failure / COPD / cirrhosis / CKD / diabetes etc. Driver for frailty score.

questions.label: Charlson Comorbidity Index
questions.helper: 0-1 fit, 2-3 intermediate, ≥4 frail (for elderly + solid tumours).

questions.label: G8 Geriatric Screening (0-17)
questions.helper: ≤14 — full geriatric assessment required before intensive chemotherapy.

questions.label: Child-Pugh class (liver)
questions.helper: Only if cirrhosis / liver disease present. B/C — excludes most regimens.

questions.label: Rapid progression (rapid volume increase / new symptoms)?
questions.helper: Driver for intensification in aggressive lymphomas / CAR-T bridging.

questions.label: New metastatic disease on repeat scan?
Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy (CTCAE)
questions.helper: Grade ≥3 — bortezomib SC, vincristine, oxaliplatin, cisplatin contraindicated.

questions.label: NYHA class (heart failure)
questions.helper: Only if HF history present. NYHA III/IV — anthracyclines / trastuzumab contraindicated.

questions.label: WBC (×10⁹/L)
questions.helper: Hyperleukocytosis (>50-100) — risk of leukostasis in AML/ALL/CML-blast.

questions.label: Uncontrolled hypertension?
questions.helper: Contraindicated / use with caution with VEGF inhibitors (bevacizumab, sunitinib, lenvatinib).

notes: STUB auto-generated 2026-04-27. Required: clinician review of biomarker question wording, defaults, missing disease-specific risk factors.

title: Multiple Myeloma — newly diagnosed (1L)
intro: Questionnaire for newly-diagnosed multiple myeloma. The 1L algorithm distinguishes standard-risk (VRd triplet) vs high-risk cytogenetics (D-VRd quadruplet, if daratumumab is available). 8-10 minutes to complete.

groups.title: Demographics + transplant eligibility
groups.description: Age + fitness drive transplant-eligible vs ineligible track.

questions.label: Age
questions.helper: Age ≤70 + ECOG ≤2 without severe comorbidities = transplant-eligible. Engine will not propose autoSCT consolidation if age >75.

questions.label: Sex
questions.helper: Women of reproductive age — negative beta-hCG required + two contraceptive methods before lenalidomide (REMS).

questions.label: ECOG Performance Status
questions.helper: ECOG > 2 → transplant-ineligible track, doses are reduced.

questions.label: Candidate for autoSCT (transplant-eligible)?
triggers: select:induction → autoSCT consolidation (4-6 cycles VRd instead of continuous)
questions.helper: Hematologist's clinical judgment — NOT automatically derived from age. Takes into account comorbidities, cardiac/renal function, performance.

groups.title: Cytogenetic risk (defining for VRd vs D-VRd)
groups.description: FISH panel (minimum del17p / t(4;14) / t(14;16) / 1q gain) — driver of selection.

questions.label: MM cytogenetics composite — high-risk?

options.label: High-risk (≥1 of: del17p, t(4;14), t(14;16), gain 1q)

questions.helper: High-risk → engine selects D-VRd (quadruplet with daratumumab) as default instead of VRd. Standard-risk → VRd remains.

questions.label: del(17p) by FISH
questions.helper: Worst single cytogenetic marker; ~10% NDMM.

questions.label: t(4;14) (IgH-FGFR3)
questions.helper: ~15% NDMM; bortezomib partially mitigates.

questions.label: Gain 1q (≥3 copies)
questions.helper: +1q = additional adverse marker.

questions.label: TP53 mutation (NGS)
questions.helper: TP53 mutation with or without del17p — poor prognosis.

groups.title: Renal function
groups.description: MM-induced renal failure is common and affects dosing.

questions.label: Creatinine clearance (Cockcroft-Gault)
questions.helper: CrCl <40 → bortezomib without adjustment, lenalidomide cuts to 10 mg. CrCl <30 → daratumumab in monitoring window.

questions.label: Active MM-induced renal failure?
questions.helper: Cast nephropathy or light-chain proximal tubulopathy — consider VTd as bridge.

groups.title: Safety: pregnancy + neuropathy + HBV
groups.description: Contraindications that block specific drugs.

questions.label: Pregnancy / possibility of pregnancy

options.label: Not pregnant / not of reproductive age

questions.helper: Pregnancy — absolute contraindication to lenalidomide. Engine blocks both VRd and D-VRd, falls back to bortezomib + dex (Vd).

questions.label: Pre-existing neuropathy (CTCAE grade)
questions.helper: Grade ≥3 — bortezomib SC contraindicated. Engine falls back to carfilzomib-based induction (Kd, KCd, KRd).

questions.label: HBsAg
triggers: RF-HBV-COINFECTION (especially with D-VRd — anti-CD38 + dex highly immunosuppressive)
questions.helper: HBV reactivation risk on D-VRd is even higher than with rituximab. Engine requires entecavir prophylaxis.

groups.title: Bone disease + supportive care indicators
groups.description: Does not change the algorithm, but adds supportive care components.

questions.label: Lytic bone lesions on CT/MRI/PET?
questions.helper: >2 lytic lesions = bone-modifying agent obligatory ≥2 years.

questions.label: History of thrombosis or high VTE risk?
questions.helper: Lenalidomide + dex baseline VTE risk ~10-20%. High-risk → LMWH instead of aspirin 81 mg.

groups.title: General screening (optional)
groups.description: Universal fields for fitness assessment, infection status, and organ function. Fill in whatever is in the chart — empty fields are simply not evaluated by the engine (replaces 'unevaluated RedFlag' warnings).

questions.label: LVEF (%) — echocardiography
questions.helper: Before anthracyclines / HER2 agents / alkylating agents — baseline LVEF required.

questions.label: Active tuberculosis?
questions.helper: Screening before IO / steroids / alloSCT. Quantiferon or screening chest X-ray.

questions.label: Active uncontrolled infection?
questions.helper: Any sepsis / pneumonia / uncontrolled fungal infection — treatment delay required.

questions.label: Total bilirubin (× ULN)
questions.helper: Most regimens require bili ≤1.5×ULN; FDA approvals commonly express this in multiples of ULN.

questions.label: DLCO (% of predicted)
questions.helper: DLCO <50% — bleomycin contraindicated (cHL ABVD); <40% — caution with CAR-T.

questions.label: QTc (ms) on ECG
questions.helper: QTc >480 → caution with venetoclax, crizotinib, apalutamide, FLT3i.

questions.label: Uric acid
questions.helper: Elevated level + bulky disease → TLS risk, rasburicase required.

questions.label: Number of significant comorbidities
questions.helper: Heart failure / COPD / cirrhosis / CKD / diabetes etc. Driver for frailty score.

questions.label: Charlson Comorbidity Index
questions.helper: 0-1 fit, 2-3 intermediate, ≥4 frail (for elderly + solid tumours).

questions.label: G8 Geriatric Screening (0-17)
questions.helper: ≤14 — full geriatric assessment required before intensive chemotherapy.

questions.label: Child-Pugh class (liver)
questions.helper: Only if cirrhosis / liver disease present. B/C — excludes most regimens.

questions.label: Rapid progression (rapid volume increase / new symptoms)?
questions.helper: Driver for intensification in aggressive lymphomas / CAR-T bridging.

questions.label: New metastatic disease on repeat scan?
Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy (CTCAE)
questions.helper: Grade ≥3 — bortezomib SC, vincristine, oxaliplatin, cisplatin contraindicated.

questions.label: NYHA class (heart failure)
questions.helper: Only if HF history present. NYHA III/IV — anthracyclines / trastuzumab contraindicated.

questions.label: WBC (×10⁹/L)
questions.helper: Hyperleukocytosis (>50-100) — risk of leukostasis in AML/ALL/CML-blast.

questions.label: Uncontrolled hypertension?
questions.helper: Contraindicated / use with caution with VEGF inhibitors (bevacizumab, sunitinib, lenvatinib).

notes: STUB — pending Clinical Co-Lead approval per CHARTER §6.1.

questions.helper: Engine routes through this biomarker if positive. Source: KB BIO-NTRK-FUSION.

questions.label: PD-L1 Tumor Proportion Score (TPS)
questions.helper: Source: KB BIO-PDL1-TPS.

questions.label: RET alterations (fusion or activating point mutation)

questions.label: ROS1 fusion

questions.label: Tumor mutational burden (TMB-high)

groups.title: Baseline safety (virology + blood)
groups.description: Screening before systemic therapy. Engine uses this for RF-HBV-REACTIVATION-RISK etc.

questions.label: HBsAg
questions.helper: HBsAg+ → entecavir prophylaxis before anti-CD20/anti-CD38/cytotoxic immunosuppressive.

groups.title: Additional parameters
groups.description: Fill in what is available; the rest — engine will fall back to safe defaults.

questions.label: Creatinine clearance (CrCl, mL/min)
questions.helper: <30 → renal-impaired regimens; <60 → dose modify many cytotoxics.

questions.label: Bilirubin × ULN
questions.helper: >1.5× ULN → hepatic-impairment regimens.

questions.label: ANC (K/μL)
questions.helper: <1.0 K/μL — engine will add G-CSF prophylaxis.

questions.label: Platelets (K/μL)
questions.helper: <75 — many regimens dose-modify; <50 — hold most cytotoxics.

groups.title: General screening (optional)
groups.description: Universal fields for fitness assessment, infection status, and organ function. Fill in whatever is in the chart — empty fields are simply not evaluated by the engine (replaces 'unevaluated RedFlag' warnings).

questions.label: LVEF (%) — echocardiography
questions.helper: Before anthracyclines / HER2 agents / alkylating agents — baseline LVEF required.

questions.label: Active tuberculosis?
questions.helper: Screening before IO / steroids / alloSCT. Quantiferon or screening chest X-ray.

questions.label: Active uncontrolled infection?
questions.helper: Any sepsis / pneumonia / uncontrolled fungal infection — treatment delay required.

questions.label: Total bilirubin (× ULN)
questions.helper: Most regimens require bili ≤1.5×ULN; FDA approvals commonly express this in multiples of ULN.

questions.label: DLCO (% of predicted)
questions.helper: DLCO <50% — bleomycin contraindicated (cHL ABVD); <40% — caution with CAR-T.

questions.label: QTc (ms) on ECG
questions.helper: QTc >480 → caution with venetoclax, crizotinib, apalutamide, FLT3i.

questions.label: Uric acid
questions.helper: Elevated level + bulky disease → TLS risk, rasburicase required.

questions.label: Number of significant comorbidities
questions.helper: Heart failure / COPD / cirrhosis / CKD / diabetes etc. Driver for frailty score.

questions.label: Charlson Comorbidity Index
questions.helper: 0-1 fit, 2-3 intermediate, ≥4 frail (for elderly + solid tumours).

questions.label: G8 Geriatric Screening (0-17)
questions.helper: ≤14 — full geriatric assessment required before intensive chemotherapy.

questions.label: Child-Pugh class (liver)
questions.helper: Only if cirrhosis / liver disease present. B/C — excludes most regimens.

questions.label: Rapid progression (rapid volume increase / new symptoms)?
questions.helper: Driver for intensification in aggressive lymphomas / CAR-T bridging.

questions.label: New metastatic disease on repeat scan?
Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy (CTCAE)
questions.helper: Grade ≥3 — bortezomib SC, vincristine, oxaliplatin, cisplatin contraindicated.

questions.label: NYHA class (heart failure)
questions.helper: Only if HF history present. NYHA III/IV — anthracyclines / trastuzumab contraindicated.

questions.label: WBC (×10⁹/L)
questions.helper: Hyperleukocytosis (>50-100) — risk of leukostasis in AML/ALL/CML-blast.

questions.label: Uncontrolled hypertension?
questions.helper: Contraindicated / use with caution with VEGF inhibitors (bevacizumab, sunitinib, lenvatinib).

notes: STUB auto-generated 2026-04-27. Required: clinician review of biomarker question wording, defaults, missing disease-specific risk factors.

groups.description: Base fields. ECOG 4 → engine routes to palliative track.

questions.label: ECOG Performance Status
questions.helper: ECOG 4 → engine will not propose active treatment.

questions.label: Line of therapy
questions.helper: This questionnaire targets 1L. For 2L+ use the extended JSON mode.

groups.title: Biomarkers (auto-extracted from indications)
groups.description: Fields auto-generated from biomarker_requirements in disease Indications. To be refined by clinician.

questions.label: BIO-KI67-PROLIFERATION
questions.helper: Source: KB BIO-KI67-PROLIFERATION.

groups.title: Baseline safety (virology + blood)
groups.description: Screening before systemic therapy. Engine uses this for RF-HBV-REACTIVATION-RISK etc.

questions.label: HBsAg
questions.helper: HBsAg+ → entecavir prophylaxis before anti-CD20/anti-CD38/cytotoxic immunosuppressive.

groups.title: Additional parameters
groups.description: Fill in what is available; the rest — engine will fall back to safe defaults.

questions.label: Creatinine clearance (CrCl, mL/min)
questions.helper: <30 → renal-impaired regimens; <60 → dose modify many cytotoxics.

questions.label: Bilirubin × ULN
questions.helper: >1.5× ULN → hepatic-impairment regimens.

questions.label: ANC (K/μL)
questions.helper: <1.0 K/μL — engine will add G-CSF prophylaxis.

questions.label: Platelets (K/μL)
questions.helper: <75 — many regimens dose-modify; <50 — hold most cytotoxics.

groups.title: General screening (optional)
groups.description: Universal fields for fitness assessment, infection status, and organ function. Fill in whatever is in the chart — empty fields are simply not evaluated by the engine (replaces 'unevaluated RedFlag' warnings).

questions.label: LVEF (%) — echocardiography
questions.helper: Before anthracyclines / HER2 agents / alkylating agents — baseline LVEF required.

questions.label: Active tuberculosis?
questions.helper: Screening before IO / steroids / alloSCT. Quantiferon or screening chest X-ray.

questions.label: Active uncontrolled infection?
questions.helper: Any sepsis / pneumonia / uncontrolled fungal infection — treatment delay required.

questions.label: Total bilirubin (× ULN)
questions.helper: Most regimens require bili ≤1.5×ULN; FDA approvals commonly express this in multiples of ULN.

questions.label: DLCO (% of predicted)
questions.helper: DLCO <50% — bleomycin contraindicated (cHL ABVD); <40% — caution with CAR-T.

questions.label: QTc (ms) on ECG
questions.helper: QTc >480 → caution with venetoclax, crizotinib, apalutamide, FLT3i.

questions.label: Uric acid
questions.helper: Elevated level + bulky disease → TLS risk, rasburicase required.

questions.label: Number of significant comorbidities
questions.helper: Heart failure / COPD / cirrhosis / CKD / diabetes etc. Driver for frailty score.

questions.label: Charlson Comorbidity Index
questions.helper: 0-1 fit, 2-3 intermediate, ≥4 frail (for elderly + solid tumours).

questions.label: G8 Geriatric Screening (0-17)
questions.helper: ≤14 — full geriatric assessment required before intensive chemotherapy.

questions.label: Child-Pugh class (liver)
questions.helper: Only if cirrhosis / liver disease present. B/C — excludes most regimens.

questions.label: Rapid progression (rapid volume increase / new symptoms)?
questions.helper: Driver for intensification in aggressive lymphomas / CAR-T bridging.

questions.label: New metastatic disease on repeat scan?
Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy (CTCAE)
questions.helper: Grade ≥3 — bortezomib SC, vincristine, oxaliplatin, cisplatin contraindicated.

questions.label: NYHA class (heart failure)
questions.helper: Only if HF history present. NYHA III/IV — anthracyclines / trastuzumab contraindicated.

questions.label: WBC (×10⁹/L)
questions.helper: Hyperleukocytosis (>50-100) — risk of leukostasis in AML/ALL/CML-blast.

questions.label: Uncontrolled hypertension?
questions.helper: Contraindicated / use with caution with VEGF inhibitors (bevacizumab, sunitinib, lenvatinib).

notes: STUB auto-generated 2026-04-27. Required: clinician review of biomarker question wording, defaults, missing disease-specific risk factors.

questions.helper: This questionnaire targets 1L. For 2L+ use the extended JSON mode.

groups.title: Baseline safety (virology + blood)
groups.description: Screening before systemic therapy. Engine uses this for RF-HBV-REACTIVATION-RISK etc.

questions.label: HBsAg
questions.helper: HBsAg+ → entecavir prophylaxis before anti-CD20/anti-CD38/cytotoxic immunosuppressive.

groups.title: Additional parameters
groups.description: Fill in what is available; the rest — engine will fall back to safe defaults.

questions.label: Creatinine clearance (CrCl, mL/min)
questions.helper: <30 → renal-impaired regimens; <60 → dose modify many cytotoxics.

questions.label: Bilirubin × ULN
questions.helper: >1.5× ULN → hepatic-impairment regimens.

questions.label: ANC (K/μL)
questions.helper: <1.0 K/μL — engine will add G-CSF prophylaxis.

questions.label: Platelets (K/μL)
questions.helper: <75 — many regimens dose-modify; <50 — hold most cytotoxics.

groups.title: General screening (optional)
groups.description: Universal fields for fitness assessment, infection status, and organ function. Fill in whatever is in the chart — empty fields are simply not evaluated by the engine (replaces 'unevaluated RedFlag' warnings).

questions.label: LVEF (%) — echocardiography
questions.helper: Before anthracyclines / HER2 agents / alkylating agents — baseline LVEF required.

questions.label: Active tuberculosis?
questions.helper: Screening before IO / steroids / alloSCT. Quantiferon or screening chest X-ray.

questions.label: Active uncontrolled infection?
questions.helper: Any sepsis / pneumonia / uncontrolled fungal infection — treatment delay required.

questions.label: Total bilirubin (× ULN)
questions.helper: Most regimens require bili ≤1.5×ULN; FDA approvals commonly express this in multiples of ULN.

questions.label: DLCO (% of predicted)
questions.helper: DLCO <50% — bleomycin contraindicated (cHL ABVD); <40% — caution with CAR-T.

questions.label: QTc (ms) on ECG
questions.helper: QTc >480 → caution with venetoclax, crizotinib, apalutamide, FLT3i.

questions.label: Uric acid
questions.helper: Elevated level + bulky disease → TLS risk, rasburicase required.

questions.label: Number of significant comorbidities
questions.helper: Heart failure / COPD / cirrhosis / CKD / diabetes etc. Driver for frailty score.

questions.label: Charlson Comorbidity Index
questions.helper: 0-1 fit, 2-3 intermediate, ≥4 frail (for elderly + solid tumours).

questions.label: G8 Geriatric Screening (0-17)
questions.helper: ≤14 — full geriatric assessment required before intensive chemotherapy.

questions.label: Child-Pugh class (liver)
questions.helper: Only if cirrhosis / liver disease present. B/C — excludes most regimens.

questions.label: Rapid progression (rapid volume increase / new symptoms)?
questions.helper: Driver for intensification in aggressive lymphomas / CAR-T bridging.

questions.label: New metastatic disease on repeat scan?
Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy (CTCAE)
questions.helper: Grade ≥3 — bortezomib SC, vincristine, oxaliplatin, cisplatin contraindicated.

questions.label: NYHA class (heart failure)
questions.helper: Only if HF history present. NYHA III/IV — anthracyclines / trastuzumab contraindicated.

questions.label: WBC (×10⁹/L)
questions.helper: Hyperleukocytosis (>50-100) — risk of leukostasis in AML/ALL/CML-blast.

questions.label: Uncontrolled hypertension?
questions.helper: Contraindicated / use with caution with VEGF inhibitors (bevacizumab, sunitinib, lenvatinib).

notes: STUB auto-generated 2026-04-27. Required: clinician review of biomarker question wording, defaults, missing disease-specific risk factors.

questions.helper: Most regimens require bili ≤1.5×ULN; FDA approvals commonly express this in multiples of ULN.

questions.label: DLCO (% of predicted)
questions.helper: DLCO <50% — bleomycin contraindicated (cHL ABVD); <40% — caution with CAR-T.

questions.label: QTc (ms) on ECG
questions.helper: QTc >480 → caution with venetoclax, crizotinib, apalutamide, FLT3i.

questions.label: Uric acid
questions.helper: Elevated level + bulky disease → TLS risk, rasburicase required.

questions.label: Number of significant comorbidities
questions.helper: Heart failure / COPD / cirrhosis / CKD / diabetes etc. Driver for frailty score.

questions.label: Charlson Comorbidity Index
questions.helper: 0-1 fit, 2-3 intermediate, ≥4 frail (for elderly + solid tumours).

questions.label: G8 Geriatric Screening (0-17)
questions.helper: ≤14 — full geriatric assessment required before intensive chemotherapy.

questions.label: Child-Pugh class (liver)
questions.helper: Only if cirrhosis / liver disease present. B/C — excludes most regimens.

questions.label: Rapid progression (rapid volume increase / new symptoms)?
questions.helper: Driver for intensification in aggressive lymphomas / CAR-T bridging.

questions.label: New metastatic disease on repeat scan?
Pre-existing peripheral neuropathy (CTCAE)
questions.helper: Grade ≥3 — bortezomib SC, vincristine, oxaliplatin, cisplatin contraindicated.

questions.label: NYHA class (heart failure)
questions.helper: Only if HF history present. NYHA III/IV — anthracyclines / trastuzumab contraindicated.

questions.label: WBC (×10⁹/L)
questions.helper: Hyperleukocytosis (>50-100) — risk of leukostasis in AML/ALL/CML-blast.

questions.label: Uncontrolled hypertension?
questions.helper: Contraindicated / use with caution with VEGF inhibitors (bevacizumab, sunitinib, lenvatinib).

notes: STUB auto-generated 2026-04-27. Required: clinician review of biomarker question wording, defaults, missing disease-specific risk factors.
